etymology.description: semantic 牛(牛) (niú, "ox") + phonetic 寺(寺) (sì) -> large bull
etymology.interpretation: The character 特(tè, "special") combines the semantic component 牛(牛) (niú, "ox"), representing strength and significance, with the phonetic component 寺(寺) (sì), which contributes the sound. The meaning 'special' might derive from the metaphor of a notably large bull, indicating something out of the ordinary or exceptional.

examples.chinese: 这是一种特别的经历(這是一種特別的經歷)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè shì yī zhǒng tèbié de jīnglì.

examples.chinese: 他的声音在演讲中显得特别清晰(他的聲音在演講中顯得特別清晰)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de shēngyīn zài yǎnjiǎng zhōng xiǎndé tèbié qīngxī.
examples.english: His voice sounded particularly clear during the speech.

examples.chinese: 为了这个特殊的场合，我们准备了一些特别的菜肴(為了這個特殊的場合，我們準備了一些特別的菜餚)。
examples.pinyin: Wèile zhège tèshū de chǎnghé, wǒmen zhǔnbèi le yīxiē tèbié de càiyáo.
examples.english: For this special occasion, we prepared some special dishes.
